hero.label: Our Story
hero.heading: Changing lives, room by room, since 2012.
hero.lead: Sunshine on a Ranney Day transforms the lives of children with special needs by creating dream spaces that bring comfort, joy, and independence — all at no cost to families.

founders.label: How It Began
founders.heading: A sermon, a calling, and a room that changed everything.
paragraphs: In 2012, inspired by a church sermon, Peter and Holly Ranney felt a deep calling to use their God-given talents to serve others. With a vision rooted in love, purpose, and creativity, Sunshine on a Ranney Day was born — founded on the belief that every child deserves a space that reflects their personality, brings them joy, and supports their unique needs.

The very first makeover was for 11-year-old Mathew, who dreamed of spending his final days in a military-themed bunker bedroom. That room became more than just a space — it became a sanctuary. His story profoundly moved the Ranneys and solidified the impact that a thoughtfully designed room could have on a child's life and family.
It was the beginning of something much bigger.

turningPoint.label: A National Moment
turningPoint.heading: Then the world met Tripp.
paragraphs: Soon after, the world was introduced to Tripp Halstead, a young boy whose story captured hearts across the country after a traumatic brain injury. When Sunshine on a Ranney Day completed a whole-home makeover for Tripp, 1.6 million people tuned in to watch the reveal live on WSB-TV.

The national attention helped shine a light on the mission and expand the reach of the organization. Tripp's joyful spirit and incredible resilience left a lasting legacy — and further inspired the work still being done today.

milestones.title: The First Room
milestones.desc: Inspired by a church sermon, Peter and Holly Ranney build their first room — a military-themed bedroom for 11-year-old Mathew.

milestones.title: Tripp Halstead
milestones.desc: A whole-home makeover for Tripp captures 1.6 million viewers on a WSB-TV livestream, launching SOARD into the national spotlight.

milestones.title: The Today Show
milestones.desc: Peter and Holly appear on NBC's Today Show for their renovation of parent sleep rooms at Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.

milestones.title: Braves Community Heroes
milestones.desc: Holly and Peter are honored as Atlanta Braves Community Heroes at SunTrust Park alongside Dansby Swanson and Freddie Freeman.

milestones.title: Sunny & Ranney Opens
milestones.desc: SOARD opens a nonprofit home furnishings store in Roswell, with 100% of profits funding room makeovers for kids.

milestones.title: Women Making a Mark
milestones.desc: Atlanta Magazine honors Holly as one of their "Women Making a Mark" for her impact on the community.

milestones.title: 100+ Rooms
milestones.desc: SOARD crosses the 100-room milestone, having served over 130 children and impacted 17,000+ through community projects.

mascot.label: Our Mascot
mascot.heading: Meet Gracie the Pig, the heart and spirit of SOARD.
paragraphs: Gracie was inspired by an unforgettable wedding gift — when Peter surprised Holly with a baby pot-bellied pig named Gracie. What started as a unique and heartfelt gesture has since grown into something magical.

Over the years, Gracie has transformed into our golden, high-flying mascot, soaring above the clouds in her signature cape, always on the lookout for children and families in need of a little extra sunshine.

With her trusty handsaw and a heart full of hope, Gracie leads her team of Sunshiners to build wheelchair-accessible bathrooms, dream bedrooms, and in-home therapy spaces — spaces that bring comfort, independence, and joy to children facing daily challenges.
mascot.tagline: She's a reminder that with creativity, compassion, and a whole lot of heart, anything is possible with a little bit of Sunshine.

store.label: Sustaining the Mission
store.heading: Shopping that builds dream rooms.
paragraphs: In 2020, Sunshine on a Ranney Day took a bold step to sustain its mission by opening Sunny & Ranney, a nonprofit home furnishings and décor store in Roswell, GA.
The shop features all brand-new, designer home goods and furniture, with 100% of the profits going directly to fund custom renovations for children with special needs.

today.label: Where We Are Today
today.heading: What began as a simple idea has become a movement.
today.body: Thanks to the unwavering support of donors, partners, and customers, Sunshine on a Ranney Day has grown into a community-driven force for good — touching the lives of more than 17,000 children and families across the greater Atlanta area. What started as a single room has become a beacon of hope, bringing comfort, dignity, independence, and joy to children who deserve nothing less than a space to thrive.

finale.heading: Be part of the next chapter.
finale.body: Every donation, every volunteer hour, every shared story builds another dream room for a child who needs it most.
